join_order.notes: Anti-join probe into pre-built bucket hash index. Use two-stage group prefetching to hide both memory latency levels (bucket_offsets + entries).

scan.access_pattern: sequential within qualifying blocks, two-phase: filter then group-prefetch probe+aggregate

data_structures.uom_dict: load dictionary to find USD code
data_structures.group_prefetch_buffer: Per-thread batch buffer of BATCH_SIZE=32 pending probes. Each entry stores: row index (uint32_t), sub_fk/tag_code/version_code (uint32_t×3), hash (uint64_t), bucket (uint64_t), bStart (uint64_t), bEnd (uint64_t). Stack-allocated struct array.
data_structures.aggregation: Custom FlatHashMap struct with: (1) keys array of uint64_t initialized to 0 as sentinel via MAP_ANONYMOUS, (2) parallel vals array of AggState {int64_t cnt; double total;}, (3) occupied_slots vector<uint32_t> tracking indices of written slots. Size = 131072 slots (power of 2). Key encoding: ((tag_code<<32)|version_code)+1 to avoid sentinel collision. Linear probing with multiplicative hash. On insert of NEW key, push slot index to occupied_slots.
data_structures.selection_vector: thread-local uint32_t sel[100000] holding qualifying row indices within each block

parallelism.aggregation_strategy: Each thread owns one FlatHashMap with occupied_slots tracking. After scan, merge by iterating only occupied_slots of each thread-local map.

indexes.purpose: restrict scan to USD rows only

optimization_notes: Category D (micro-architecture optimization): main_scan=29ms dominates. Root cause: 9M anti-join probes into 153MB pre index (3.5× LLC) with TWO dependent cache misses per probe — first to read bucket_offsets[bucket], second to read pre_entries[bStart..bEnd]. The iter_3 code prefetches bucket_offsets 8 rows ahead but NEVER prefetches the entries array, leaving the second (dependent) cache miss fully latency-bound at ~100ns each. Fix: implement two-stage group prefetching (amortized hash probing) with BATCH_SIZE=32. Process qualifying rows in batches of 32: Stage 1 computes all 32 hashes and issues __builtin_prefetch for bucket_offsets[bucket] for each; Stage 2 reads all 32 bucket offset pairs (bStart, bEnd) and issues __builtin_prefetch for &pre_entries[bStart] for each; Stage 3 performs the actual entry scan and match check for all 32, aggregating anti-join survivors. This hides BOTH levels of dependent memory latency by separating them into distinct loops with 32 prefetches in flight simultaneously. The tail (selCount % 32 remaining rows) is processed with the same pattern. DO NOT implement a Bloom filter — analysis shows it is ineffective for this anti-join pattern where 99% of rows match (Bloom positive requires full index probe for correctness, saving only ~1% of probes). Keep all other structures unchanged: FlatHashMap with occupied_slots, two-phase scan (pass 1 filter, pass 2 probe), zone map block skipping. Expected main_scan reduction: 29ms → 12-18ms from hiding the entries cache miss latency.
